//: Example CENTRALLY SERVED orchestrator catalog — try central config mode against this file as served by GitHub:
//use: set MCP_ORCHESTRATOR_CONFIG_URL=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Byggarepop/dotnet-mcp-orchestrator/main/docs/orchestrator.central.example.json in the orchestrator's env block, restart your MCP host once, and the catalog below goes live (then updates within the poll interval, default 300 s).
//rules: A central catalog must work on every consuming machine: no ${CONFIG_DIR} or ${SOLUTION_DIR} (validation rejects them). Use ${ENV_VAR} — resolved on each machine, which also keeps secrets out of this shared file — or absolute paths.
//servers: The entries below launch via npx (needs Node.js) or dotnet tool execute (needs the .NET SDK), so they work on any machine. Downstream servers start lazily on first use; the generous connectTimeoutSeconds covers the package download on a first run.

capabilities.name: tokensaver
capabilities.summary: Token-efficient source reading for .NET/web codebases: outline C#/VB files (signatures + line ranges), minify whole files of many languages, trace DI registrations.

capabilities.name: everything
capabilities.summary: MCP reference server with demo tools (echo, add, longRunningOperation, sampling and more) — a safe playground to route to.

capabilities.name: files
capabilities.//: ${USERPROFILE} resolves on each consuming machine (Windows). On macOS/Linux, change it to ${HOME}.
capabilities.summary: Read and inspect files under the user's home directory (list, read, search).
